inline_comments.message: nope. please put this as an option in your values file
are you sure that you want to share this with cds-blueprints?
please don't try to access port based on its position in a list it's very fragile. Use helper macro prepared by Sylavin
are you sure? If yes then please put a comment in a code and clarify why you share this PV
good for testing but we won't merge it with staging
it's rather conf. you shouldn't name section by the template that will be using it. Think about this file that it's your "user interface"
do you recommend some linter for helm charts that would catch those? They are annoying to search manually before commit :(
Yes, the cds-blueprints folder is used at the moment for shared storage of blueprint zip files

I will consult with Yuriy to be sure.

EDIT: Yes, confirmed.
Will update to 0.7.0, it should be present in nexus soon.
ok then please put a comment here with some explanation for next generations;)
we have a template to have consistent labeling throughout ONAP, please use it (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/103421 as an example)

we have a template to have consistent labeling throughout ONAP, please use it (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/103421 as an example
you'll also need a selector for apps/v1 but a template also exists

is it sensitive data?
If yes, please use "common.secret" template
is this "AUTH_TOKEN" sth that comes from the other service or it's sth that you define here?

use common.templateMetadata
you also needs selectors (common.selectors) with apps/v1
please use our templates and be on apps/v1 (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/104168 is a good one to see)

don't forget the indentation
I personnaly prefer "ports: {{ include "common.containerPorts" . | nindent 12 }}"
I personally prefer resources: {{ include "common.resources" . | nindent 12 }}

nope nope nope nope nope my eyes are bleeding... don't kill me please...

You should store your username and password separately and then just concatenate them to a single env variable by overriding your docker entrypoint (cmd)
who is going to use this password?
Sorry, the review is old and I missed the version change 👍 I will fix that
Same 😉 I missed a version change on master. Will correct that.
Yes, I didn't see the update on master on last rebase. I will bump that 👍

Will do! 👍
Why is the slower implementation kept in the code? Is secretFast not 1:1 when it comes to functionality?

Can you elaborate on "by overriding your docker entrypoint (cmd)"? I tried to prepare a token from username and password while setting up env in deployment.yaml but I didn't find a way to do it properly.
it's here so we can create secrets api-credentials and api-auth-token. Then we read those secrets to set up env variables in deployment.yaml. I'm not sure I understand the question 😞
The only reason is the trick that we do in mariadb-init but I'll rework that shortly;)
Checkout modeling/charts/modeling-etsicatalog/templates/deployment.yaml and you will have a ready pattern how to deal with this;)

The question is if this is the passwords that someone needs to know to access your service or this is a password that you need to know to access some other service? In other words from the perspective of those credentials are you a server or client?
py-executor is a client. The login and password are so we can access blueprint processor api.
cool. It would be really awesome if you could add that in a comment to above those credentials;) it will simplify our work in a future;)

would be great if you could add support for externalSecret (see all other patches).
It will be unused for now but we want to use more and more of that in a future
put this line just at the end of line 33 (like for line 17)
Line 77 already has that. Do I need to add it also somewhere else?
Oh no it's my fault I just missed the line 77. Thanks for letting me know
